content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

All four amendments identify real defects verified against the code (src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs, src/adapters/br_process.rs, src/cli/milestone.rs) and against the project prompt.md spec that explicitly requires parent-child hierarchy edges and idempotent reruns.

## Votes

- `fr-2-44f5298d`: Accept
  Verified: ensure_dependency (service.rs:1987-2003) calls BrMutationAdapter::add_dependency, which builds BrCommand::dep_add(from,to) (br_process.rs:415-417, 1372-1388) without --type, so br defaults to 'blocks'. The project prompt.md explicitly specifies parent-child for root->workstream (line 46) and workstream->task (line 56) hierarchy edges, while BeadProposal.depends_on edges are blocks. The current code uses blocks for all edges, which violates the contract and produces a hierarchy downstream code keying on DependencyKind::ParentChild (as seen in src/cli/project.rs:1290, 1684-1685) will not recognize as epic parenting. Remediation of threading kind through the mutation API is correct.
- `fr-2-02669566`: Accept
  Verified: handle_export_beads (cli/milestone.rs:371-372) constructs BrMutationAdapter::with_adapter, which in br_process.rs:817-818 assigns a fresh Uuid::new_v4() adapter_id per invocation. exec_tracked_mutation_locked (1246-1286) clears the pending record on failure only when own_record_existed was false at the start, so any successful mutation followed by a later failure leaves a persisted record in .beads/.br-unsynced-mutations/<UUID>.json. ensure_bead_export_preflight (service.rs:1823-1834) calls ensure_synced, and ensure_synced_locked (1107-1121) refuses on ANY dirty record regardless of ownership. The next export-beads run uses UUID2, recover_pending_mutation_state flags it as dirty, and preflight unconditionally fails — no in-run recovery path. Real idempotency/recoverability defect.
- `fr-2-28ce7798`: Accept
  Verified: workstream description commenting (service.rs:1588-1600) and per-bead planning comment (1637-1654) are both gated on workstream_epic.created / bead.created. materialize_bead_entry (1731-1763) returns created=false whenever find_existing_materialized_bead returns a reuse. Given create_bead persists to the local DB immediately while comment_bead is a separate mutation, a mid-run failure after create but before comment leaves the bead reusable on retry but the comment permanently skipped — yet the rerun completes successfully. The spec's acceptance criterion 'Planning rationale attached as comments' (prompt.md line 103) requires the comment be present on the exported bead, so silently dropping it is a real defect. Remediation to detect/replay missing comments or persist enough state to replay them is appropriate.
- `fr-2-e87ed6c1`: Accept
  Verified: root epic labels are [milestone-root, milestone:<id>] (service.rs:1548-1551) while workstream epic labels are only [milestone:<id>] (1570). summary_matches_materialized_bead (1870-1878) uses subset containment on labels, not equality, and detail_matches_materialized_bead (1880-1888) mirrors that. existing_by_title buckets by normalized title (1515-1529), and bundle.rs:141-158 does not reject a workstream whose name equals bundle.identity.name. In that valid bundle, on rerun both the root epic and the like-named workstream epic land in the same title bucket; the root query's input matches only the root (eligible=1), but matches.len()==2 triggers the ambiguity branch at 1937-1952 and fails with 'ambiguous because conflicting matches also exist'. The command is therefore not idempotent on a valid bundle. Remediation via disjoint identity criteria (require/exclude milestone-root) or bundle validation is correct.
